## What this changes

<!-- One or two sentences. Which dated claim(s), attribution(s), or source(s) does this add or correct? -->

## Sourcing checklist

- [ ] Every new or changed dated claim carries a **source URL** a stranger can open and check.
- [ ] For each, I've noted **how the date was established** (git author-date, Wayback timestamp, standard's publication date, docket filing date…).
- [ ] The source **resolves right now** and actually supports the claim (not a publish date or recollection standing in for one).
- [ ] If I added a source, I updated the bit-rot protection in [`evidence/`](../blob/main/evidence/README.md) — or flagged in this PR that it still needs archiving.
- [ ] If this overturns the earliest root (`2014-07-23`), I've linked an artifact that provably predates it.
- [ ] I understand contributions are dedicated to the public domain under [CC0 1.0](../blob/main/LICENSE).

## Notes for the maintainer

<!-- Anything to double-check: an ambiguous date, a source that may rot, a judgment call. -->
